mnemonics.title: BULLY Always Wins
mnemonics.content:
B - Broadcast election
U - Uphold highest ID
L - Listen for OK
L - Look for coordinator
Y - Yield to highest

mnemonics.title: Ring Election Steps
mnemonics.content:
C - Collect IDs in circle
I - Identify highest
R - Return to initiator
C - Confirm new leader
L - Link neighbors (reconfigure)

acronyms.usage: Message sent by processes to indicate they are alive during election
Message initiated to start leader election process
Message broadcast to inform all processes about new leader

visualTips.visualization: Think of a classroom where the oldest student (highest ID) becomes class monitor. When monitor leaves, everyone checks if older student is present - the oldest takes over.

Imagine a round table discussion. Each person passes a note with their name. The note collects all names, and whoever has the 'highest status' name when the note returns becomes the discussion leader.
Think of a heartbeat monitor in a hospital. The leader sends regular 'pulses'. If pulses stop, nurses (other processes) realize and call for a new shift supervisor.

analogies.analogy: Like selecting a team captain in a sports team - the player with the highest jersey number (or seniority) automatically becomes captain when needed.
Like passing a token in a circular seating arrangement during a meeting - the token collects preferences and returns to the starter who announces the decision.
Like an air traffic control tower - one central authority coordinates all planes (distributed processes) to ensure safe and orderly operations.
Like friends waiting for a late arriver at a restaurant - after a certain time (timeout), they assume the person isn't coming and proceed without them.
